question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `floorEntry(blockNumber)` lookup in `getEvmVersionForBlock()` with a crafted block number that is between two version boundaries to consistently select an older EVM version, enabling exploitation of version-specific gas calculation differences to under-report gas usage?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hierarchical fallback in `getEvmVersions()` — where an empty `evmVersions` TreeMap causes fallback to `network.evmVersions`, and an empty `network.evmVersions` causes fallback to `DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP` — to trigger the default map path on TESTNET/PREVIEWNET/OTHER networks, causing all historical calls to use `EVM_VERSION` (v0.67) regardless of the actual block's historical EVM version?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TESTNET, PREVIEWNET, and OTHER `HederaNetwork` enum values have `Collections.emptyNavigableMap()` for `evmVersions`, causing `getEvmVersions()` to always fall back to `DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP` for historical calls on those networks, applying the latest EVM version to all historical blocks and bypassing version-specific security constraints?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable `evmVersions` TreeMap field (initialized as `new TreeMap<>()` at line 62) — if it is not properly protected — to inject or remove EVM version mappings at runtime, causing `getEvmVersions()` to return attacker-controlled version mappings for subsequent requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: validatePayerBalance] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default value of `validatePayerBalance = false` at line 98 to submit contract calls with a `value` or `gasPrice` greater than zero from an account with zero balance, bypassing the payer balance check and executing value transfers without sufficient funds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: validatePayerBalance] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `validatePayerBalance = false` (default) to submit a contract call with a large `value` field from a zero-balance address, causing the EVM to simulate a transfer that would be rejected on-chain, producing misleading execution results that could be used to deceive off-chain systems?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: validatePayerBalance] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `validatePayerBalance = false` (default) combined with `minimumAccountBalance = 100_000_000_000_000_000L` to make any account appear to have a large balance for non-balance opcodes, enabling contract logic that checks `address.balance` to be deceived into believing an attacker-controlled address is well-funded?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: minimumAccountBalance] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `minimumAccountBalance = 100_000_000_000_000_000L` default in `getBalanceOrDefaultToMinimum()` — applied when `validatePayerBalance` is false and the entity is not a CONTRACT — to make any EOA appear to hold 0.1 HBAR for non-balance opcode purposes, causing contracts that gate logic on `BALANCE` opcode results to behave incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: minimumAccountBalance] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `minimumAccountBalance` floor applied to non-balance opcode calls to make a newly created or zero-balance account appear funded, bypassing contract-level balance guards and enabling unauthorized execution paths within smart contracts that check `msg.sender.balance`?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: maxGasLimit] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with a gas limit exactly equal to `maxGasLimit = 15_000_000L` repeatedly in parallel, exploiting the disabled gas throttle (`contracts.throttle.throttleByGas=false`) to consume maximum CPU resources per request and cause sustained denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: maxGasLimit] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Min(21_000L)` constraint on `maxGasLimit` — which only enforces a minimum, not a maximum cap beyond the default — to observe that if `maxGasLimit` is misconfigured to a very large value, they can submit gas-limit requests that exhaust server resources without any upper bound enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: maxGasRefundPercentage] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `maxGasRefundPercentage = 100` (default, maximum allowed) to submit contract calls that consume minimal gas but claim the maximum refund, causing the gas accounting to return nearly all submitted gas and enabling effectively free contract execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: maxGasRefundPercentage] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Max(100) @Positive` constraints on `maxGasRefundPercentage` — which allow a value of 100 — combined with `contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit` being set from this field in `buildTransactionProperties()`, to receive a 100% gas refund on every call, making all contract calls effectively free and enabling unlimited execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: maxGasEstimateRetriesCount] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `eth_estimateGas` request for a contract that always fails, causing `BinaryGasEstimator.search()` to iterate up to `maxGasEstimateRetriesCount = 20` times per request, and by submitting many such requests in parallel, cause CPU exhaustion and denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: maxGasEstimateRetriesCount] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `maxGasEstimateRetriesCount = 20` default in `BinaryGasEstimator.search()` — where each iteration executes a full EVM call — to craft a contract that maximizes per-iteration cost, causing each `eth_estimateGas` request to perform 20 full EVM executions and amplifying resource consumption by 20x?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: estimateGasIterationThresholdPercent] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `estimateGasIterationThresholdPercent = 0.10d` in `BinaryGasEstimator.search()` — where the threshold is computed as `(long)(lo * 0.10)` — by supplying a very small initial `lo` value (e.g., `lo = 21000`) to produce a threshold of `2100`, causing the binary search to always exhaust all `maxGasEstimateRetriesCount` iterations and maximizing CPU consumption per estimate request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: estimateGasIterationThresholdPercent] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the floating-point computation `(long)(lo * properties.getEstimateGasIterationThresholdPercent())` in `BinaryGasEstimator` — where `lo` is attacker-controlled via the gas parameter — to cause integer overflow or precision loss that produces a threshold of `0`, causing the early-exit condition `Math.abs(prevGasLimit - mid) < estimateIterationThreshold` to never trigger and always running the maximum iteration count?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: entityNumBuffer] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `entityNumBuffer = 1000L` default — which adds a buffer to the max known entity number to prevent ID conflicts — by observing that a very small buffer could allow contract creation simulations to generate entity IDs that collide with entities being concurrently persisted by the importer, causing state corruption or incorrect contract execution results?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: entityNumBuffer] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `entityNumBuffer` field — which is only validated as `@Positive` with no upper bound — to observe that if misconfigured to a very large value, entity ID generation could overflow `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing arithmetic errors in entity ID assignment during contract creation simulations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: evmVersion] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `evmVersion` is used directly in `buildTransactionProperties()` at line 152 to set `contracts.evm.version`, and that `buildTransactionProperties()` is lazily initialized — meaning the version is frozen at first access — to observe that a race condition during startup could cause the wrong EVM version to be baked into `transactionProperties` for the lifetime of the application?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: evmVersions] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable `evmVersions = new TreeMap<>()` field — which is not declared as unmodifiable — to observe that if a reference to this map is obtained and mutated concurrently, `getEvmVersions()` could return inconsistent version mappings during concurrent historical block queries, causing incorrect EVM version selection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `network = HederaNetwork.TESTNET` default — where TESTNET has `Collections.emptyNavigableMap()` for `evmVersions` — to observe that all historical block queries on TESTNET fall back to `DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP` (always `EVM_VERSION` v0.67), causing historical simulations to use the latest EVM semantics regardless of the actual block's historical EVM version?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `HederaNetwork.OTHER` enum value with `chainId = 0x012A` — which is a fixed, publicly known chain ID — to craft replay attacks by signing transactions for the `OTHER` network and replaying them against a mirror node configured with `network=OTHER`, bypassing chain ID-based replay protection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: transactionProperties] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of `transactionProperties` via `@Getter(lazy = true)` — which uses double-checked locking internally — to trigger a race condition during the first concurrent access, causing `buildTransactionProperties()` to be called multiple times and producing inconsistent property maps used by different threads?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: versionedConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of `versionedConfiguration` at line 95-96 — which calls `new ConfigProviderImpl(false, null, getTransactionProperties()).getConfiguration()` — to observe that if `getTransactionProperties()` returns a map containing attacker-injected properties (via the `properties` override), those properties are permanently baked into the `VersionedConfiguration` used for all subsequent EVM executions?
